name: Garbanzo Parsnip Gnocchi
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the coarsely chopped parsnips, finely diced onions, and minced garlic. Stir until the onions are translucent and the parsnips are soft, about 10-12 minutes.
3. Transfer the mixture to a food processor and blend until a smooth paste forms.
4. Add the garbanzo flour, gluten flour, nut yeast, salt, and white pepper to the food processor. Blend until well combined.
5. Heat peanut oil in the skillet to 375°F (190°C).
6. Using a spoon, scoop small portions of the batter into the hot oil. Be careful not to overcrowd the pan; fry no more than four gnocchi at a time.
7. Turn off the heat and allow the gnocchi to cook until golden brown, about 1 minute per side.
8. Remove the gnocchi with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
9. For tomato sauce: Make your basic homemade tomato sauce without bay leaf and add extra red wine if desired.
Serves 4
